Tests the behavior of Math.pow with negative infinity and negative exponents.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests a mathematical operation involving `Math.pow` and `Infinity` in JavaScript. It checks if dividing 1 by the result of raising negative infinity to the power of -5 equals negative infinity. This assertion is used to verify the behavior of the `Math.pow` function when handling special numeric values like infinity, ensuring that the JavaScript engine correctly implements this mathematical rule. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical edge case.
